goal: Make /app/contacts/new feel like a real relationship-source intake flow while keeping camera, scan, import, email, calendar, referral, and merge behavior simulated and side-effect-free.

success_criteria.description: GET /app/contacts/new first viewport leads with one current contact review candidate, the source that created it, the next decision, and the no-side-effect boundary before alternate intake paths.

Manual note, business card, QR, event attendee import, external contacts, email/calendar signals, referral, and merge paths are presented as selectable source methods with readable labels, status, and next step copy rather than a long debug list.

Readable source chips or labels are visible in the main flow, while raw IDs such as evidence:*, source:*, queue:*, uppercase error codes, and implementation slugs stay only inside details, hidden diagnostics, or test evidence.

GET /app/contacts/new?scenario=empty, pending, and failure preserve meaningful recovery states with visible recovery controls; desktop and 375px mobile evidence show no horizontal overflow, unlabeled controls, or text escaping its container.

Page and route-flow tests assert the source-intake narrative, selectable source methods, primary action safety, raw-ID containment, and route-state recovery; npm test and npm run build pass with no feature, service, API, data fixture, shared UI, or unrelated route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing feature contracts, services, service factories, API envelopes, route handlers, or sample data fixtures
Adding live camera, OCR, address book, email, calendar, CRM, notification, messaging, storage, or network integrations
Changing the global app shell, global CSS, or shared UI primitives
Implementing contacts list, contact detail, event, follow-up, chat, dashboard, or agent route polish reserved for later sprints
